data.title: Freehold commercial building at 562 Serangoon Road on sale via EOI
data.description:
The EOI will close on 9 July.

Brilliance Capital announced the sale of a freehold commercial building with individual strata titles per floor at 562 Serangoon Road via an expression of interest (EOI).

The four-storey commercial property has a land area of approximately 2,336 sq ft with a total floor area of approximately 7,453 sq ft. It has a plot ratio of 3.0.

The property has a guide price of $23.5m.

Zoned “Commercial”, the ground floor of this non-conserved commercial asset is approved for food and beverage (F&B) use.

Subject to authorities’ approvals, the property could also be used for serviced apartments, co-living residences, lifestyle clinics, co-working spaces, or creative concept showrooms.

The property is located near the Farrer Park MRT Station and Boon Keng MRT Station (North-East Line). It is also well connected to major expressways, including the CTE and PIE.

In addition, it is near popular areas such as Little India, Farrer Park, Bidadari, and the Kallang Riverside precinct.

The EOI closes on Wednesday, 9 July at 3:00 p.m.

data.title: Manulife US REIT reports lower occupancy rate in Q1
data.description:
The REIT recently sold a property for $173.55m.

Manulife US REIT reported a 69.9% occupancy rate in Q1 2025, a dip from 73.9% in the previous quarter.

The group attributed the dip in occupancy to to lease expiries at Diablo, located in Tempe, Arizona.

The dip was offset by recently signed leases at Phipps at Centerpointe.

Rent reversion further dropped to -8.9% from -5.1% in Q4 2024.

The group also reported that it sold Peachtree, a 28-storey Class A office building in Atlanta, Georgia, for $173.55m (US$133.8m) to repay 58% of its 2026 debts.

data.title: Franklin Templeton gets approval to launch first retail tokenised fund in Singapore
data.description:
Investors will be able to access the fund with a minimum investment of US$20.

Franklin Templeton has secured approval from the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) to launch its first retail tokenised fund in the country.

The Franklin OnChain US Dollar Short-Term Money Market Fund aims to offer investors exposure to a portfolio of high quality short-term securities, consisting principally of transferable securities and money market instruments of governments.

It will also consist of eligible securities of companies of any nation worldwide, primarily US dollar denominated or hedged back into the US dollar.

Investors will be able to access the fund with a minimum investment of US$20.

The fund is structured as a sub-fund under the Franklin Templeton Investments VCC.

The fund will be managed by Templeton Asset Management Ltd, which in turn appointed Franklin Advisers Inc as sub-managers.

data.title: SGX securities market turnover rises to $40.6b in April
data.description:
SDAV also rose 59% YoY to $1.9b.

The Singapore Exchange (SGX Group) reported a 59% year-on-year (YoY) rise in the securities daily average value (SDAV) to $1.9b in April.

This increase lifted the total securities market turnover value to $40.6b.

In the derivatives segment, traded volume increased 24% YoY to 29.9 million contracts on record foreign exchange (FX) futures activity, whilst derivatives daily average volume (DAV) rose 24% YoY to 1.5 million contracts.

In addition, Singapore was the most-traded ASEAN cash market in April, with its 2025 SDAV growing 22% year-to-date (YTD).

Notably, the Straits Times Index (STI) outperformed other ASEAN benchmarks YTD with a 1.2% price return and 2.9% total return.

data.title: Singapore firms deploy AI to counter shrinking margins
data.description:
The government now expects GDP growth as low as 0-2% in 2025.

Singapore’s economy is under strain from U.S. tariffs, domestic slowdowns, and shrinking funding. Tech startup funding in 2024 fell to just $2.1 billion—a 56% plunge from 2023—while the Ministry of Trade and Industry slashed the country’s 2025 GDP growth forecast to a narrow 0.0% to 2.0%.

In this environment, companies are intensifying their use of enterprise AI, process automation, and cloud-based platforms to reduce costs, stretch workforce capacity, and maintain competitiveness.

“What's ahead of us now is having industries in the right domains looking at this as a fantastic opportunity to pivot,” said Terry Smagh, Senior Vice President and General Manager, Asia Pacific and Japan, Infor. “Most importantly, also the optimisation measures that they can take to further their business.”

According to Infor’s How Possible Happens survey, 81% of Asia Pacific organisations agree that deploying technologies such as robotic process automation and process mining is key to increasing responsiveness and visibility—two traits crucial to navigating volatility.

“These are really faster to respond to turbulent markets, but most importantly, also provide a platform that can help you scale,” said Smagh.

He stressed that Singapore businesses must prioritise three core capabilities: building repeatable optimisation processes, ensuring platforms can scale globally, and embedding industry-specific tools that address actual bottlenecks.

“It’s not only just about lowering costs… but really spreading alternative technologies that's agile… to improve customer satisfaction,” he added.

data.title: URA proposes five sites for conservation
data.description:
This will be part of the Draft Master Plan 2025, one of the key SG60 programmes.

Minister for National Development, Desmond Lee announced that the Urban Redevelopment Authority (URA) will be proposing to conserve significant buildings at five sites as part of the upcoming Draft Master Plan 2025 (DMP2025), one of the key SG60 programmes.

These sites are 

  • The former Royal Malayan Navy (RMN) Admin Block
  • Jurong Hill Tower
  • Former SIT blocks at Dakota Crescent
  • The Grandstands at Bukit Timah Turf City, and
  • Singapore Badminton Hall

According to the URA, the sites and buildings reflect significant aspects of Singapore’s development and collectively, the story of Singapore’s transformation as an independent nation and underscores URA’s strong, continued commitment to conservation.

The URA said it will continue to work with stakeholders and partner organisations on other ways to recall significant heritage that contributes to the country’s collective history and culture.

This includes heritage interpretation in various forms, such as through digital documentation, physical markers like storyboards and walking trails, and sensitive urban design, to retain a sense of place and character.

URA will be presenting the proposal to conserve the relevant buildings at these five sites at the DMP2025 public exhibition later this year. Further plans for our built heritage will be announced at the same time.

data.title: Cariflex inaugurates new $461m latex plant in Singapore
data.description:
The plant supports growing demand in markets such as non-surgical medical gloves.

Cariflex Pte. Ltd. (Cariflex), a subsidiary of DL Chemical, has inaugurated its new $461m  (US$355m) polyisoprene latex plant at Jurong Island, Singapore.

Spanning 6.1 hectares, the plant supports growing demand in markets such as non-surgical medical gloves, adhesives, and laminates.

The plant is designed for modular expansion to accommodate future demand growth. When fully ramped up, it will double Cariflex's 2023 manufacturing capacity for polyisoprene latex.

“Singapore's thriving economy, along with a highly skilled workforce and strong intellectual property protection, made this the ideal choice for our investment,” Ryu Sang Woo, Chief Executive Officer at Cariflex, said.

The facility has created 80 new permanent roles, the majority filled by Singaporeans. 

($1=US$0.77)

data.title: AEM posts 71.4% QoQ net profit drop in Q1
data.description:
Net profit fell from $11.7m to $3.3m.

AEM Holdings (AEM) posted a 71.4% quarter-on-quarter (QoQ) drop in net profit from $11.7m to $3.3m in Q12025. 

Revenue for the period fell 35.1% QoQ to $86.0m from $132.6m in Q4 2024.
 
This decline was due to the pull-in of orders by one of the group’s customers from FY2025 into H2 2024.

This was partially offset by high volume manufacturing at its major new Artificial Intelligence (“AI”) / High-Performance Compute (“HPC”) anchor customer.

AEM’s Test Cell Solutions (TCS) received twice as many customers compared to the previous quarter, following its deployment of the automated burn-in system AMPS-BI).

The AMPS-BI system received customer acceptance, with multiple units transitioned into volume production.

data.title: Sinarmas Land defends IFA valuation in Lyon Investment privitisation offer
data.description:
SGXRegCo is questioning how the IFA derived the 20% to 22% holding discount figure.

Sinarmas Land defended the valuation methods of W Capital Markets Pte. Ltd, the independent financial advisor (IFA) appointed to advise the company’s Independent Directors in the privatisation deal with Lyon Investment in response to queries raised by the Singapore Exchange Regulation (SGXRegCo)

The government regulator raised two queries in response to the IFA Letter and its 6 May 2025 update on the offer.

SGXRegCo questioned how the IFA derived the 20% to 22% holding company discount and asked for both the supporting details and comparable examples from past SGX-listed offers.

In a bourse filing, Sinarmas Land said the IFA based the 20% to 22% discount on the mean and median discounts, 19.5% and 24%, respectively, of 11 SGX-listed mid to large-cap conglomerates, including property firms, as of 27 March 2025, citing companies such as CapitaLand Investment, City Developments, and Wee Hur Holdings.

“In addition, it is noted from the above that the Property Conglomerates has an implied holding company discount of approximately 24%. In view of the above analysis, W Capital Markets has applied a holding company discount range of 20% to 22%, which it deems to be a reasonable range to adopt,” Sinarmas Land said.

data.title: Golden Agri-Resources net profit jumps 47% in Q1
data.description:
It was driven by higher crude palm oil (CPO) prices.

Golden Agri-Resources Ltd (GAR) reported a 47% surge in net profit to $71.48m (US$55m) in the first quarter of 2025.

The increase was driven by higher crude palm oil (CPO) prices, which rose 27% to an average of $1,503 per tonne (US$1,156), and improved plantation output.

Revenue grew 19% to $3.95b (US$3.04b) despite lower sales volumes. EBITDA rose 12% to $336.52m (US$259m) with an 8.5% margin, whilst underlying profit increased to $115.57m (US$89m).

CEO Franky Widjaja said palm oil production is recovering after extreme weather disruptions, which may ease supply tightness. However, geopolitical uncertainty and trade tensions will continue to affect market conditions, supporting CPO prices in the near term.
